paper_focus.title: Effect of cold rolling and subsequent heat treatment on microstructural evolution and mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C-(Ni) based austenitic low-density steels
paper_focus.research_objective: To elucidate the synergistic effects of Mn/Ni ratio and thermo-mechanical processing (cold rolling and subsequent annealing) on concurrent precipitation of κ-carbide/B2 particles and recrystallization behavior, and to establish processing windows for optimizing strength-ductility combinations in Fe-Mn-Al-C-(Ni) low-density steels.
main_process_variables: Cold rolling to 50% reduction in thickness at room temperature
Heat treatment in temperature range 973–1173 K for 1 h followed by water quenching

visual_evidence.description: This figure presents engineering stress-strain curves for Fe-Mn-Al-C-(Ni) based austenitic low-density steels, specifically comparing three alloy compositions: 28Mn0Ni (Fe-28Mn-9Al-0.9C), 28Mn5Ni (Fe-28Mn-9Al-0.9C-5Ni),...
